brief_justification: Exact correct answer: A→B→D→E→F→H, 105 minutes (1h45m); Lists all three competing chains and traces second-order merge-point effects explaining why each non-critical branch fails to gate; No explicit reflection on reasoning method or named biases, proceeds linearly; Cross-checks both alternative chains at merge points (B 9:30 > G 9:15, E→F gates later than C→F) confirming the critical path; Presents conclusion with full confidence and no acknowledgment of assumptions or edge cases; Systematically evaluates both alternative chains (A→G→D→E→F→H and C→F→H) with specific time-based rejection reasoning; Every node has an explicit timestamp and merge-point logic is fully stated, allowing third-party reproduction.
